title: Generate INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md before dispatching parallel Morty teams
source_context: Claude Code /insights analyzed 150+ sessions and identified this as the highest-ROI fix for parallel orchestration. Quote: 'Having agents agree on shared types/interfaces before coding would prevent most post-deploy firefighting.' Our team.py has context_file but not a strict integration contract step. [PRIORITY: /insights report identified as top-ROI fix]
description: From /insights (Claude Code usage report): #1 root cause of 'mostly_achieved' sessions requiring hotfix rounds is parallel-agent integration bugs — API contract mismatches, missing files, CORS double-escaping, queue routing. Fix: before spawning parallel Mortys (team-mode), Rick generates a shared INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md file containing: (1) API endpoint signatures with request/response types, (2) shared env vars, (3) file locations each agent writes to, (4) queue/event names, (5) shared TypeScript interfaces / JSON schemas. Then inject this file path as MANDATORY first read into each Morty's system prompt. Team-mode already has shared context; formalize it as a first-class contract file that teammates must ingest before any code is written.
proposed_changes: Add scripts/team.py command 'write-contract' that generates .cto/teams/<team-id>/INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md from the epic ticket. Extend build_prompt() in delegate.py for team members to include: 'FIRST: read INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md from team context — do NOT write code that violates these signatures.' Gate team start on contract existence.
